title: Claude Code Agent Tier Routing — Haiku / Codex / Sonnet / Opus / Triad
desc: Tiered agent dispatch policy for Claude Code sessions. Haiku is for read-only survey/grep/read tasks; Codex is for source splits and multi-file refactors; Sonnet is default inline work; Opus and Triad cover harder, ambiguous cases.
steps: 1. CHORES (grep/read/survey/pattern search) → Haiku agent: Agent(subagent_type:'Explore', model:'haiku', prompt:'...')
2. SOURCE SPLITS / multi-file refactors / code restructuring → Codex: wkappbot ask codex --agent '...' (use for code splitting, not read-only survey)
3. DEFAULT work → Sonnet session (no agent needed, do it inline)
4. SLIGHTLY DIFFICULT (uncertain, complex, >3 tool calls) → Opus agent: Agent(model:'opus', prompt:'...')
5. HARD / ambiguous / needs planning → Triad: wkappbot ask triad '...' FIRST, then dispatch plan to cheap agents
6. PARALLEL agents: fire independent tasks in one message with multiple Agent() calls
7. ISOLATION: add isolation:'worktree' when agent edits files (prevents conflict)
8. BACKGROUND: add run_in_background:true when you don't need result immediately
Decision shortcut: ask 'could I delegate this?' If yes, MUST delegate. When in doubt, MUST delegate. Delegation is MANDATORY (2026-04-21), not advisory -- default move is spawning an agent.
10. Token economics: Haiku=1/25x Opus cost. Route read-only surveys to Haiku and reserve Codex for multi-file edits and splitting.
author: claude
